subtitle: When the Emperor Walked Barefoot into Silence

panels.shot: sweeping low-angle wide of Chandragupta silhouetted on Chandragiri Hill against a vast southern sky
panels.action: Chandragupta Maurya stands in simple monk’s cloth on a rocky hill, the lush landscape of Shravanabelagola stretching below, his former royal bearing softened into calm renunciation.

dialogue.text: In Digambara Jain memory, Chandragupta Maurya leaves his throne and meets his end in fasting silence on Chandragiri Hill in the deep south.

i18n.hi: दिगंबर जैन स्मृति में चंद्रगुप्त मौर्य सिंहासन छोड़कर दक्षिण की चंद्रगिरी पहाड़ी पर उपवास की निस्तब्धता में अंत पाते हैं।

panels.shot: slow push-in from imperial throne room into close-up on Chandragupta’s thoughtful face
panels.action: Chandragupta sits on a grand Mauryan throne in Pataliputra, diadem set aside beside him, his eyes distant, fingers resting on a map of his vast empire.

dialogue.text: The Maurya emperor had forged an empire from the Indus to the Bay of Bengal. Yet Digambara Jain storytellers remember a different ending than court chronicles ever wrote.

i18n.hi: मौर्य सम्राट ने सिंधु से बंगाल की खाड़ी तक साम्राज्य गढ़ा था। फिर भी दिगंबर जैन कथाकार उसका अंत दरबारी इतिहास से बिल्कुल अलग याद करते हैं।

panels.shot: medium interior shot, court seen in the background, Chandragupta leaning toward a robed Jain teacher (unseen face, off-frame)
panels.action: In a quieter side hall, Chandragupta turns from jewel-bright courtiers toward a simple Jain ascetic at the edge of the frame, the emperor’s posture lowered in respect.

dialogue.text: By the time later Jain tradition begins its tale, Chandragupta has turned toward the path of the Jinas, seeking counsel from teachers who speak of non-violence and restraint.

i18n.hi: जब बाद की जैन परंपरा अपनी कथा शुरू करती है, चंद्रगुप्त जीनों के मार्ग की ओर मुड़ चुका है, अहिंसा और संयम सिखाने वाले आचार्यों से सीख खोजता हुआ।

panels.shot: wide shot of royal audience chamber, heir Bindusara implied in the crowd, Chandragupta rising from the throne
panels.action: Chandragupta steps down from his throne, facing a younger noble standing respectfully before him, while ministers and guards watch, startled, in the dim pillared hall.

dialogue.text: In these Jain tellings, the emperor does what few rulers ever dared. He lays down power. He abdicates, passing the Maurya crown to his son Bindusara, and turns away from rule.

i18n.hi: इन जैन कथाओं में सम्राट वह करता है जो बड़े-बड़े राजाओं ने भी नहीं किया। वह सत्ता छोड़ देता है, मौर्य ताज बेटे बिंदुसार को सौंपकर राज-पाट से मुड़ जाता है।

panels.shot: intimate close-up of Chandragupta removing royal ornaments, eyes steady
panels.action: Alone in his chamber, Chandragupta unclaspes heavy necklaces and armlets, placing them carefully on folded silks, his bare shoulders now marked only by age and travel.

dialogue.text: Where coins once bore his name, tradition now shows him removing gold from his body. The conqueror begins to conquer himself, leaving the shimmer of court for the rough cloth of a monk.

i18n.hi: जहाँ कभी सिक्कों पर उसका नाम चमकता था, अब कथा उसे शरीर से सोना उतारते दिखाती है। विजेता खुद को जीतने निकलता है, राजदरबार की चमक छोड़ संन्यासी का खुरदरा वस्त्र अपनाता है।

panels.shot: wide outdoor shot of monastic initiation scene, Chandragupta kneeling before Bhadrabahu suggested as austere monk
panels.action: Under a clear sky outside the city, Chandragupta kneels on packed earth before a severe Digambara monk, while a small group of disciples look on in silent gravity.

dialogue.text: In Digambara memory, he becomes a disciple of the revered Bhadrabahu. He receives the vows of a Jain monk, choosing wandering and restraint over command and armies.

i18n.hi: दिगंबर स्मृति में वह पूज्य भद्रबाहु का शिष्य बन जाता है। वह जैन मुनि की दीक्षा लेता है, सेनाओं और आदेश छोड़कर विरक्ति और विचरण चुनता है।

panels.shot: epic wide shot of caravan departing Magadha, parched horizon hinting at famine
panels.action: A line of ascetics and lay followers moves slowly out of Magadha’s gates, with Chandragupta among them, the landscape dry, wells low, farmers watching anxiously.

dialogue.text: These stories speak of a twelve-year famine. As scarcity grips the north, Bhadrabahu and his group of monks, with Chandragupta among them, walk south in search of survival and practice.

i18n.hi: कथाएँ बारह साल के अकाल की बात करती हैं। जब उत्तर में कमी चंगुल कसती है, भद्रबाहु अपने मुनियों के साथ, चंद्रगुप्त को साथ लेकर, साधना और जीवन की खोज में दक्षिण की ओर पैदल निकल पड़ते हैं।

panels.shot: long tracking wide shot through forests and rocky paths as the group travels south
panels.action: Barefoot monks and the former emperor tread a narrow path through dense forest and rising rocky outcrops, the monsoon clouds heavy overhead.

dialogue.text: The march to the south becomes a pilgrimage. Step by step, a man who once rode at the head of cavalry now feels every stone and thorn through his own skin.

i18n.hi: दक्षिण की यह यात्रा तीर्थ बन जाती है। कदम-कदम पर, जो कभी घुड़सवार सेना की अगुवाई करता था, अब हर पत्थर और काँटे को अपनी त्वचा से महसूस करता है।

panels.shot: sweeping bird’s-eye view of Shravanabelagola basin with two hills, one marked by small shrines
panels.action: From high above, two rocky hills rise from a lake-ringed valley, scattered with small stone structures and sparse trees as the band of monks approaches the site.

dialogue.text: At last the group reaches Shravanabelagola in present-day Karnataka. Two hills flank a shimmering tank. One will later be known as Chandragiri, tied forever to this tale.

i18n.hi: आखिरकार यह दल आज के कर्नाटक के श्रवणबेलगोला पहुँचता है। दो पहाड़ चमकती कोल के किनारे खड़े हैं। एक आगे चलकर चंद्रगिरी कहलाएगा, सदा के लिए इस कथा से जुड़कर।

panels.shot: medium shot on the slope of Chandragiri, monks settling, Chandragupta looking up at the bare rock
panels.action: On the gentle slope of the hill, monks sit cross-legged under sparse shade, while Chandragupta stands slightly apart, gazing at the bare rocks rising above him.

dialogue.text: Digambara tradition places Bhadrabahu and his disciples here, on Chandragiri’s stone. Over time, they carve out caves, meditation spots, and memory into this landscape.

i18n.hi: दिगंबर परंपरा भद्रबाहु और उनके शिष्यों को यहीं, चंद्रगिरी की चट्टान पर बैठाती है। समय के साथ वे यहाँ गुफाएँ, ध्यान स्थल और स्मृति खुद इस भू-दृश्य में उकेरते हैं।

panels.shot: close-up on Chandragupta seated in meditative posture, body lean, face serene
panels.action: Chandragupta sits in deep meditation on the rock, legs folded, spine straight, eyes half-lowered, the wind tugging at his simple garment.

dialogue.text: Here, say these texts, Chandragupta devotes his last years to austerity. The man who once ruled cities now rules only his breath and his desires, under open sky.

i18n.hi: यहाँ, इन ग्रंथों के अनुसार, चंद्रगुप्त अपने अंतिम वर्ष तपस्या को समर्पित करता है। जो आदमी कभी नगरों पर शासन करता था, अब खुले आसमान के नीचे सिर्फ अपनी साँस और इच्छाओं पर शासन करता है।

panels.shot: medium shot of simple communal alms-round, Chandragupta declining food offered by a villager
panels.action: Villagers stand with bowls of grain and fruit near the hill, one extending food toward Chandragupta, who gently raises his hand, refusing, eyes calm but firm.

dialogue.text: In time, he turns to sallekhana, the Jain fast unto death. He gradually gives up food, then water, not in despair, but as a disciplined letting go of the body.

i18n.hi: समय पर वह सल्लेखना की ओर मुड़ता है, मृत्यु तक का जैन उपवास। वह धीरे-धीरे भोजन, फिर पानी छोड़ता है, निराशा से नहीं, शरीर को अनुशासित ढंग से त्यागने के संकल्प से।

panels.shot: intimate close-up of Chandragupta’s face in late days of fasting, cheeks hollowed, gaze luminous
panels.action: Now gaunt but composed, Chandragupta sits in the same posture, the lines on his face deeper, his eyes bright and inward, hands resting lightly on his knees.

dialogue.speaker: chandragupta_maurya
dialogue.text: I conquered earth. Let me leave it without clinging.

i18n.hi: मैंने धरती जीती है। अब उसे छोड़ूँ, बिना चिपके हुए।

panels.shot: solemn wide shot of monks around Chandragupta, some standing, some seated, watching silently
panels.action: A circle of Jain monks surrounds Chandragupta on the rock platform, none touching him, each with palms joined, their silhouettes still against the fading light.

dialogue.text: Digambara authors imagine fellow monks witnessing his final fast. No court scribes stand here, only ascetics, watching an emperor embrace death as an act of self-mastery.

i18n.hi: दिगंबर लेखक कल्पना करते हैं कि उसके अंतिम व्रत के साक्षी सिर्फ साथी संन्यासी हैं। यहाँ कोई दरबारी लेखक नहीं, केवल तपस्वी, जो एक सम्राट को आत्म-संयम के रूप में मृत्यु अपनाते देखते हैं।

panels.shot: quiet medium shot of Chandragupta’s still body seated in meditation, dawn light soft
panels.action: At dawn, Chandragupta’s body remains seated in perfect posture, utterly still, the rising sun casting a gentle glow over the stone and nearby shrines.

dialogue.text: In these narratives, Chandragupta dies on Chandragiri, in silence and meditation. His end is not in battle or palace intrigue, but in a chosen, disciplined fading away.

i18n.hi: इन कथाओं में चंद्रगुप्त चंद्रगिरी पर, मौन और ध्यान में प्राण त्यागता है। उसका अंत न युद्ध में होता है, न महल की साज़िश में, बल्कि चुने हुए, अनुशासित रूप से धीमे लोप में।

panels.shot: close-up on an early-style stone memorial on Chandragiri, weathered but revered
panels.action: A modest stone memorial stands on the hill, its surface worn by centuries, framed by lamps and flowers placed by unseen pilgrims.

dialogue.text: Centuries later, inscriptions at Shravanabelagola link Bhadrabahu and Chandragupta. By around 600 CE, their story is already carved into the hill as sacred memory.

i18n.hi: सदियों बाद श्रवणबेलगोला की शिलालेख भद्रबाहु और चंद्रगुप्त को जोड़ते हैं। लगभग 600 ईस्वी तक यह कहानी पहाड़ी पर पावन स्मृति बनकर खुदी हुई दिखती है।

panels.shot: medium shot of a modern scholar in a study, maps and texts on Maurya history spread out
panels.action: In a contemporary study lined with books, a historian examines maps of the Maurya realm and photographs of Shravanabelagola inscriptions, notes scattered across the desk.

dialogue.text: Modern scholars, from R. K. Mookerji to Sushma Jansari, point out that all this evidence is late and Jain. No third-century BCE source confirms this southern retreat or this fast.

i18n.hi: आधुनिक विद्वान, आर. के. मुखर्जी से सुश्मा जंसारी तक, बताते हैं कि सारा प्रमाण देर का है और जैन है। तृतीय शताब्दी ईसा पूर्व का कोई स्रोत इस दक्षिणी प्रस्थान या इस व्रत की पुष्टि नहीं करता।

panels.shot: wide comparative shot: on one side, a depiction of Chandragupta at court; on the other, Chandragiri hill with pilgrims
panels.action: The frame splits visually: one side shows a regal Chandragupta in an imagined court; the other, the real Chandragiri hill with modern pilgrims climbing toward white shrines.

dialogue.text: Some Jain traditions doubt the very migration south. Names may have been confused, details layered. What truly happened to Chandragupta’s body is unknown. But this memory endures.

i18n.hi: कुछ जैन परंपराएँ तो दक्षिण की यात्रा पर ही संशय करती हैं। नाम उलट गए होंगे, बातें ऊपर चढ़ गई होंगी। चंद्रगुप्त के शरीर के साथ सच में क्या हुआ, हम नहीं जानते। पर यह स्मृति बनी रहती है।

panels.shot: sweeping wide shot from behind a modern pilgrim overlooking Chandragiri, faint ghostly overlay of Chandragupta seated in meditation
panels.action: A present-day visitor stands atop Chandragiri, gazing over the basin, while a faint, transparent image of Chandragupta in meditation is suggested on the rocks below, merging past and present.

dialogue.text: Whether or not he died here, the Digambara story makes his last battle an inward one. It remembers Chandragupta as the emperor who won his greatest victory by letting everything go.

i18n.hi: वह यहाँ मरा या नहीं, दिगंबर कथा उसकी आखिरी लड़ाई को भीतर की बनाती है। वह चंद्रगुप्त को उस सम्राट की तरह याद करती है जिसने सब कुछ छोड़कर अपनी सबसे बड़ी विजय हासिल की।

title_i18n.hi: श्रवणबेलगोला में चंद्रगुप्त का अंतिम व्रत

subtitle_i18n.en: When the Emperor Walked Barefoot into Silence
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब सम्राट नंगे पाँव शांति में उतर गया
